2606.16891.title: Beyond Weights and Gradients: A Taxonomy of Federated Learning Messages
2606.16891.paper_summary: Federated Learning is rapidly evolving beyond the exchange of traditional model weights and gradients, yet existing definitions fail to capture the full scope of modern payloads like synthetic data and federated analytics. This paper addresses the gap by proposing a formal mathematical definition of a federated message that accounts for both utility and privacy. We introduce a taxonomy that organizes these exchanges into three categories: model structures, statistical summaries, and data-conditioned representations. By evaluating these groups based on computational demands, communication costs, and privacy risks, we provide a clearer understanding of the trade-offs involved in decentralized training. Our review of 202 recent publications highlights a significant shift since 2021 toward diverse messaging paradigms, signaling a move away from standard deep learning updates toward more specialized information sharing. This framework provides a structured path for future research to optimize federated systems for varying hardware and security requirements.
2606.16891.paper_summary_zh: 聯邦學習正迅速發展，超越了傳統模型權重和梯度的交換，但現有的定義無法捕捉像合成數據和聯邦分析等現代有效負載的全貌。本文通過提出一個正式的數學定義來填補這一空白，該定義考慮了效用和隱私。我們引入了一個分類法，將這些交換組織為三個類別：模型結構、統計摘要和數據條件表示。通過根據計算需求、通信成本和隱私風險評估這些組別，我們提供了對去中心化訓練中涉及的權衡的更清晰理解。我們對202篇最近出版物的回顧突顯了自2021年以來在多樣化消息傳遞範式方面的顯著轉變，這標誌著從標準深度學習更新轉向更專門的信息共享。這一框架為未來的研究提供了一個結構化的路徑，以優化聯邦系統以滿足不同的硬體和安全需求。
2606.16891.author: Alvaro Javier Vargas Guerrero et.al.
2606.16891.authors: Alvaro Javier Vargas Guerrero, Xinguang Wang, Quang Manh Doan, Guy Nagels
